name: La Fortuna
summary: La Fortuna is considered one of the safest tourist destinations in Costa Rica, with excellent infrastructure and strong police presence. The town's economy relies heavily on tourism, making visitor safety a top priority for locals and authorities.
quickVerdict: La Fortuna offers an excellent safety profile for visitors to Costa Rica. Crime rates are significantly lower than the national average, and violent crime against tourists is exceptionally rare. The town's well-developed tourism infrastructure includes reliable emergency services, quality medical facilities, and experienced tour operators. While petty theft can occur at popular attractions like hot springs and waterfalls, basic precautions easily mitigate these risks. Solo female travelers consistently report feeling safe, and the warm, welcoming Tico culture adds to the sense of security.

neighborhoods.name: Central La Fortuna
neighborhoods.description: The bustling downtown area with restaurants, shops, and accommodations. Well-lit streets and frequent foot traffic make it very safe day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Arenal Observatory Area
neighborhoods.description: Upscale resort area near Arenal Volcano with luxury accommodations and excellent security. Popular with families and couples.

neighborhoods.name: Hot Springs District
neighborhoods.description: Area around Tabacon and other thermal resorts. Generally safe but watch belongings at pools and during tours.

neighborhoods.name: La Fortuna Waterfall Vicinity
neighborhoods.description: Natural attraction area with hiking trails. Safe during daylight hours but avoid isolated trails after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Rural Outskirts
neighborhoods.description: Agricultural areas and remote roads outside town. Limited lighting and emergency services. Use guided tours for activities.

neighborhoods.name: Arenal Dam Area
neighborhoods.description: Less developed area toward the dam with fewer services. Road conditions can be challenging, especially in rainy season.

scams.name: Fake Tour Operators
scams.description: Street vendors offering tours without proper licenses or insurance, potentially leading to unsafe activities or financial loss.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book tours through licensed operators listed on Visit Costa Rica website or recommended by your hotel.

scams.name: Overpriced Transportation
scams.description: Unofficial taxi drivers or transport services charging inflated rates, especially to/from airports or attractions.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official red taxis with working meters, Uber where available, or pre-arranged hotel transfers.

scams.name: Attraction Parking Scams
scams.description: Individuals claiming official parking is full and directing tourists to expensive unofficial lots near national parks.
scams.howToAvoid: Research official parking locations beforehand and verify with park authorities if in doubt.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Tricks
scams.description: Business owners using outdated exchange rates to overcharge tourists paying in US dollars.
scams.howToAvoid: Know current exchange rates, pay in colones when possible, or use credit cards with good exchange rates.

soloFemale.overview: La Fortuna is considered one of the best destinations in Costa Rica for solo female travelers. The town has a strong tourism infrastructure, friendly locals known for their Pura Vida hospitality, and excellent safety track record. Women regularly travel alone here for adventure activities, wellness retreats, and nature exploration. The compact downtown area is easily walkable, and there are numerous hostels and accommodations specifically welcoming to solo travelers.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in central La Fortuna for easy access to restaurants and services
Join group tours for activities like zip-lining and volcano hikes to meet other travelers and ensure safety
Avoid walking alone on poorly lit side streets after dark, but main areas remain vibrant in evenings
Keep valuables secure at hot springs and during outdoor activities
Connect with other solo female travelers at popular hostels and tour meeting points

nightSafety.overview: La Fortuna maintains good safety levels after dark, particularly in the central tourist area. The downtown core stays lively with restaurants, bars, and evening tours operating safely. However, side streets can become poorly lit, and rural areas outside town should be avoided at night. Tourist police maintain presence in popular areas, and most accommodations offer secure parking and good lighting.
tips: Stick to main streets and well-lit areas when walking at night
Use registered taxis or arranged transport for longer distances after dark
Avoid isolated trails, beaches, or rural roads at night
Most restaurants and bars in central area are safe for evening dining
Return to accommodations by midnight as activity levels decrease significantly after then

transport.metro: No metro system available in La Fortuna
transport.rideshare: Uber operates in limited capacity. More reliable to use official red taxis or pre-arranged transfers through hotels and tour operators.
transport.taxis: Official red taxis are safe and regulated with working meters. Always ensure meter is turned on. Orange taxis serve the airport routes. Abundant availability in town center.
transport.tips: Walking is safe and practical within central La Fortuna. For attractions and volcano areas, use licensed tour operators who provide transportation. Rental cars are available but roads can be challenging, especially to rural attractions.

customs: Greet people with a warm smile and firm handshake, or light cheek kiss among friends
Say 'Pura Vida' as both greeting and expression of the laid-back Costa Rican lifestyle
Practice 'Tico Time' - understand that social events may start later than scheduled
Wait for everyone to be served before eating, and say 'buen provecho' before starting meals
Respect personal space but expect friendly, welcoming interactions from locals
Dress modestly when visiting rural areas or religious sites
Learn basic Spanish phrases to show respect, though English is widely spoken in tourist areas

health.overview: La Fortuna has good healthcare infrastructure with two medical clinics and a full-service hospital an hour away in Ciudad Quesada. The Fortuna Medical Center provides urgent care, X-rays, and emergency services with English-speaking staff. Private healthcare is excellent quality and affordable compared to North American standards.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Costa Rica, including La Fortuna. The water treatment standards meet international requirements.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for La Fortuna. Standard travel vaccines like Hepatitis A/B recommended. Malaria risk is extremely low in this area.
health.altitude: La Fortuna sits at approximately 200 meters elevation, so altitude is not a concern. Climate is tropical with year-round temperatures 20-30°C. High humidity and frequent rain during May-November rainy season.

emergency.usEmbassy: For emergencies: +506 8863-4895 after hours. Regular hours: Oficentro Ejecutivo La Sabana, Building 7, San José

faq.q: Is La Fortuna safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, La Fortuna is considered one of the safest tourist destinations in Costa Rica. Crime rates are low, infrastructure is well-developed, and the local economy depends on tourism, making visitor safety a priority.

faq.q: Is La Fortuna safe at night?
faq.a: The central tourist area remains safe at night with good lighting and police presence. Stick to main streets and avoid isolated areas. Most restaurants and evening activities operate safely until around midnight.

faq.q: Is La Fortuna safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Absolutely. La Fortuna is highly recommended for solo female travelers with excellent infrastructure, friendly locals, and a strong community of international visitors. Many women travel here alone for adventure and wellness activities.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in La Fortuna?
faq.a: Avoid poorly lit side streets at night and isolated rural roads outside town after dark. The Arenal Dam area has limited services. Always use marked trails and avoid restricted volcano areas.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in La Fortuna?
faq.a: Official red taxis are very safe and reliable. Walking within town is safe and practical. For longer distances and attractions, use licensed tour operators or pre-arranged hotel transfers rather than public buses.
